sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were obtained with the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill.

Method-provenance block
Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic) and f⁻ (nucleophilic) per atom, plus dual descriptor and most reactive sites.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted four-center integrals were NOT used – exact ERIs as per chemkit default).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point charges on the fixed geometry → fukui from finite-difference N±1 single points).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1); N−1 and N+1 states were both doublets (charge ±1, mult 2) by default.
Software: PySCF 2.x via chemkit backend (fukui subcommand).
Convergence & caveats: all three single-point SCFs converged; integrity.trustworthy = true.  Warnings streamed by chemkit are reproduced verbatim below.

Key results (atomic indices 0-based, labels 1-based)
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, index 2) with f⁺ = 0.39938965.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.23839906.
Complete vectors are in benzaldehyde_fukui_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json together with the bar-chart PNG.

Live log: the SCF/Fukui calculation streamed to a file created at run start (path echoed by the skill); consult it for full SCF cycles and timing.

Verbatim warnings from chemkit result
– sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
– sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
– common header present
